title: ConnectionConsent
description: An AggregationLink: a single household member's independent consent grant + connection to one institution via the swappable aggregation provider (FR-CORE-006/007). Provided by Module 0 so consumers (Household, Home/onboarding, Settings) can render connection status, consent scope, and revocation WITHOUT ever touching the underlying token. The aggregation access TOKEN IS NOT, and MUST NOT be, part of this contract — tokens live only in the KMS-backed secrets store (FR-CORE-007, platform-decisions §5). This object carries only non-secret link metadata.

link_id.description: Stable id for this member's link to this institution. One link = one member + one institution + one independent token (never shared between household members — FR-CORE-007).

profile_id.description: The household member who granted this consent under their own identity. Each member holds an INDEPENDENT grant; partial revocation operates on a single link_id.

provider.description: Aggregation provider behind SpineAggregationPort. Swappable; consumers never depend on provider-specific fields (FR-CORE-006).

consent_status.description: active = healthy; login_required/reauth_required drive the repair UX (MFA-gated re-auth, FR-X-017); revoked/expired retain no live token. NEVER infers a token value.

consent_scopes.description: Least-privilege data scopes the user explicitly granted (Constitution V). The product reads only within granted scopes.

reauth_required_reason.description: Why a re-auth is needed (distinct from consent_status: this is the REASON, not the lifecycle state). login_expired here = the provider login lapsed and a fresh login is needed; it is named differently from the consent_status value `login_required` (the lifecycle state) to avoid conflation. suspected_compromise is set by the rotation triggers in the threat model (password/passkey reset, refresh-token reuse, admin-initiated, provider-signaled). Re-issuing/re-authorizing a token requires step-up MFA (FR-X-017).

revoked_by.description: household_admin_partial = partial revocation when a member leaves; invalidates only this link's token, never others' (FR-CORE-007).

token_destroyed.description: true once the secrets-store token for this link is destroyed (crypto-shred); the contract exposes only this boolean fact, never the token.

revocation.description: Present once revoked. Revocation cascades to the aggregation provider within the 7-day deletion SLA (FR-X-013).

freshness.description: Freshness of the link/connection status itself.
